de.uka.ilkd.key.informationflow.macros

Class SelfcompositionStateExpansionMacro

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.macros.AbstractProofMacro
      • de.uka.ilkd.key.macros.StrategyProofMacro
        • de.uka.ilkd.key.macros.AbstractPropositionalExpansionMacro
          • de.uka.ilkd.key.informationflow.macros.SelfcompositionStateExpansionMacro
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    ProofMacro

    public class SelfcompositionStateExpansionMacro
extends AbstractPropositionalExpansionMacro
    The macro SelfcompositionStateExpansionMacro applies rules to extract the self-composed states after the merge of the symbolic execution goals which is included in the proof obligation generation from information flow contracts. This macro splits the goal. The rules that are applied can be set in ADMITTED_RULES.

        pio - context

      • canApplyTo

        public boolean canApplyTo(Proof proof,
                          ImmutableList<Goal> goals,
                          PosInOccurrence posInOcc)
        Can this macro be applied on the given goals? This method should not make any changes but check if the macro can be applied or not on the given goals. This method may be called from within the GUI thread and be compatible with that fact. This macro can always be applied (does not change anything perhaps) TODO make this only applicable if it has an impact.

        This compound macro is applicable if and only if the first macro is applicable. If there is no first macro, this is not applicable.

        Specified by:
        canApplyTo in interface ProofMacro
        Overrides:
        canApplyTo in class StrategyProofMacro
        Parameters:
        proof - the current Proof (not null)
        goals - the goals (not null)
        posInOcc - the position in occurrence (may be null)
        Returns:
        true, if the macro is allowed to be applied

      • allowOSS

        Description copied from class: AbstractPropositionalExpansionMacro
        Whether this macro includes One Step Simplification.
